# Questions in Livestream 144

Live Stream #144: Stampede of Sacred Cows

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/soQU4CJKdt4?t=456" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">7:36</a> | What made Cain's offering unacceptable in Genesis 4? | He says the text does not blame produce as such; grain offerings were valid, so the problem is Cain's posture and failure rather than vegetables versus meat. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/soQU4CJKdt4?t=1159" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">19:19</a> | Does Matthew 24:36 refer to a Jewish wedding? | He says the passage does use wedding language, but the main point is watchfulness because the day and hour are unknown. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/soQU4CJKdt4?t=1472" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">24:32</a> | Is Kenneth Kitchen an early or late dater for the Exodus? | He answers that Kitchen is a late Exodus dater. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/soQU4CJKdt4?t=1545" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">25:45</a> | Would you consult for an Angel Studios movie on Joseph or Moses? | Yes. He says he has consulted on movies and video games before, enjoys the work, and would do it if asked. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/soQU4CJKdt4?t=1935" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">32:15</a> | Did the Egyptian word `ntr` apply only to major gods? | No. He says it could refer broadly to supernatural beings, from great gods down to lesser spirits. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/soQU4CJKdt4?t=2506" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">41:46</a> | Is Nimrod identifiable with a known Sumerian ruler? | He says the evidence is too thin for a firm one-to-one identification, so most of the discussion remains speculation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/soQU4CJKdt4?t=2687" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">44:47</a> | Can Amarna ransom prices and Joseph's slave price be used to track inflation? | He says yes, cautiously, and points to price studies showing inflation across Egyptian commodities over time. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/soQU4CJKdt4?t=2976" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">49:36</a> | How would you respond to someone saying Genesis 1:16 is wrong because the moon does not function as a light? | He says a reflected light can still function as a light source from the observer's perspective, so the objection misses the point. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/soQU4CJKdt4?t=4195" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:09:55</a> | Is "heavens" plural in Genesis 1:1, and does that include the heaven where angels dwell? | He says the Hebrew form is traditionally read as plural, but the verse does not by itself settle later distinctions about angelic heaven. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/soQU4CJKdt4?t=4645" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:17:25</a> | How do we distinguish ceremonial law from Torah? | He says Torah can include the whole Pentateuch, law generally, or ritual law; ceremonial law concerns ritual acceptability and fits under Sabbath observance. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/soQU4CJKdt4?t=5021" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:23:41</a> | Why did readers replace the divine name with Adonai? | He says it grew from a holiness concern: people came to think they were too impure to pronounce the divine name aloud. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/soQU4CJKdt4?t=5294" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:28:14</a> | Does Exodus 23:10-11 mean all Israel stopped harvesting for a whole year? | He says it points to crop rotation: one portion of land lies fallow while the rest continues producing. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/soQU4CJKdt4?t=5933" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:38:53</a> | Is Wadi al-Rummah in Saudi Arabia a good candidate for the Pishon River? | He says it is possible only if the river path fits the Genesis geography, but he has not studied the proposal deeply enough to affirm it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/soQU4CJKdt4?t=6558" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:49:18</a> | Is the Shema best translated "the Lord our God, the Lord is one" or "the Lord is our God, the Lord alone"? | He says the literal wording is "Yahweh is one," but translation choices depend on how one handles the thought and context. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/soQU4CJKdt4?t=6755" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:52:35</a> | Are there non-biblical texts mentioning the Pishon? | He says none are known to him, though the Bible mentioning rivers that later disappeared is unusual rather than unprecedented. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/soQU4CJKdt4?t=10746" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:59:06</a> | What is Christ's most important teaching in Revelation? | He summarizes Revelation's central message as "God wins," with the rest unfolding Old Testament themes around that victory. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/soQU4CJKdt4?t=10937" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:02:17</a> | What are your thoughts on the idea of Nimrod trying to hunt God? | He sees little support for that; Nimrod is portrayed more as hunting dangerous animals while civilizing Mesopotamian land. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/soQU4CJKdt4?t=11245" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:07:25</a> | Why was Avaris occupied and unoccupied if Israelites from Joseph to the Exodus were always there? | He says Avaris was not completely unoccupied; some reports focus on palaces or reduced occupation, while Semitic settlement continued. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/soQU4CJKdt4?t=11896" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:18:16</a> | How do you recognize a false teacher? | He says the best way is to know genuine Christianity and Scripture so well that counterfeits become recognizable. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/soQU4CJKdt4?t=12257" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:24:17</a> | What are your thoughts on social Darwinism and eugenics? | He calls them wicked because they deny the sacredness of human life and rest on destructive racial and genetic purity claims. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/soQU4CJKdt4?t=12918" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:35:18</a> | Is ancient firmament language a good argument for flat earth? | No. He says ancient cosmology is religious and analogical, not a physical claim that proves a flat earth. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/soQU4CJKdt4?t=13118" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:38:38</a> | What advice would you give suffering Christians who ask why they should not end life if heaven is wonderful? | He says life is an inherent good and sacred to God; deciding to shorten it is not within human authority. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/soQU4CJKdt4?t=13773" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:49:33</a> | What god does the golden calf idol represent, and does it come from Egypt? | He says it is an idol of Yahweh shaped by Egyptian cow-goddess and rebirth imagery, not a direct import of an Egyptian god. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/soQU4CJKdt4?t=13927" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:52:07</a> | What are your thoughts on antinatalism and Arthur Schopenhauer? | He says he has not read enough Schopenhauer or the antinatalist view to say much, though what he knows of Schopenhauer has not impressed him. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/soQU4CJKdt4?t=14161" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:56:01</a> | Do we need more Christian monasteries and asceticism in North America? | He sees some benefit for retreats and spiritual disciplines, but warns that monasteries are not necessarily strong teaching institutions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/soQU4CJKdt4?t=14766" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:06:06</a> | Why is there much mention of Sea Peoples conquest but no mention of the Israelite conquest? | He says even the Sea Peoples are only sparsely attested, and Egyptian records naturally emphasize threats to Egypt more than Israel's internal conquest traditions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/soQU4CJKdt4?t=15329" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:15:29</a> | What does the Bible mean by worldliness when pastors reject culture such as movies, music, or fashion? | He says "from the world" is not automatically bad; pastors often miss the distinction between being in the world and being of the world. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/soQU4CJKdt4?t=15960" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:26:00</a> | Are there two kingdoms now, or one kingdom under God? | He rejects a two-kingdom scheme here and says there is ultimately one kingdom under God's rule, with the church as its present manifestation. |
